mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 5%, or wage growth fails to accelerate despite unemployment declines.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust economic activity, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate profitability and risk appetite.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for the INDPRO-GDPC1 interaction, or 60-day returns decline despite simultaneous growth in both series.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns through increased construction sector activity and wealth effects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST rises above 2% MoM, or housing data fails to predict equity market moves.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, leading to positive S&P 500 returns through liquidity injections and risk-on sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL declines below 1.5%, or central bank policy fails to respond with liquidity measures.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k jobs signals a resilient labor market, which historically drives positive S&P 500 returns through increased consumer spending and corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k, or consumer spending fails to accelerate despite job growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) above 0.5% MoM indicates strong consumer demand, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns through improved corporate earnings and sector rotation into consumer discretionary stocks.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 0.5%, or retail data fails to predict equity market moves.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 90 signals manufacturing weakness, which historically drives negative S&P 500 returns through reduced corporate profits and sector-specific losses.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below 90, or manufacturing data fails to predict equity market moves.
